_doc: ADR-006 ratchet baseline. Counting rule: non-comment, line-leading 'record_result ' call sites in validate.sh and 'Add-Result ' in validate.ps1 (regex ^\s*record_result / ^\s*Add-Result). Helper-internal calls are a constant offset absorbed here. New checks MUST go through run_python_check / Invoke-PythonCheck (which add zero such lines). Raising a count above original_floor requires a one-line entry in 'justifications' naming the check and why it must run without Python (ADR-006 escape hatch). Lowering counts (migration) requires updating these numbers in the same change. KNOWN SCOPE LIMIT: mid-line emission styles (cmd && record_result / || { record_result } / if(){ Add-Result }) are deliberately uncounted — that authoring style for NEW checks evades the machine ratchet and is caught at review (spec Enforcement Boundary; 4 such pre-existing sites live today).
justifications: token lifecycle drift advisory (issue #157 / backlog #51): 5 native record_result + 5 native Add-Result sites emit WARN/PASS/SKIP for governance token-cost drift. Must be WARN (advisory, never FAIL); run_python_check / Invoke-PythonCheck map exit!=0 -> FAIL and cannot express WARN. Mirrors the governance-eval-coverage advisory precedent. Hard teeth live in tests/ci/test_lifecycle_baseline_drift.py.

downstream adaptability (ADR-007/008): +2 native record_result (validate.sh) and +2 Add-Result (validate.ps1) = the tool-absent SKIP branches for 'downstream-capabilities gate-safety' and 'safety nucleus freshness'. The actual checks run through run_python_check / Invoke-PythonCheck (zero added native lines); only the SKIP-when-the-deployed-tool-is-missing verdict is native, because the wrappers map a missing tool to FAIL while the correct downstream verdict on a partial/old install is SKIP (safe-to-ignore), exactly as the compact-index-freshness SKIP precedent.

AC-6 current-branch gate-evidence FAIL (dev-flow-hardening spec): +1 record_result FAIL (validate.sh) and +1 Add-Result FAIL (validate.ps1) for the single combined 'current-branch work log missing Resume block and/or Test Gate Results at handoff/ship' finding. Must be FAIL-tier native (not Python): Python check already implements the gate-progression backstop; the new finding is a complementary structural check on Resume/Test-Gate-Results presence that must emit FAIL (not WARN) to match the AC-6 spec requirement. Single combined counter avoids the count growing proportionally with sub-checks.

routing_actions stale-pending advisory (backlog #97): +1 native WARN in validate.sh and +1 Add-Result WARN in validate.ps1 because the check must work in no-python/reduced-assurance validation and emit WARN, not FAIL; the existing Python wrapper cannot express an advisory-only stale snapshot result.
